title: Red Team Lab — consolidated counter-deception lab across five authority roles

fields.vision: A consolidated counter-deception lab for adversarial review of analytical work.
fields.mechanism: It treats red-teaming as a structured discipline with five authority roles: baseline canon, implementation packet, Prism Observatory, continuation pack, and bootstrap wrapper. The lab preserves six-table SQLite schema logic, canonical JSON project schema, replayable canon, patch-and-validation records, doctrinal core, command-registry deltas, cold-start re-entry, and a lineage appendix so a reader can see which authority supports which claim.
fields.built_components: All five authority roles are present in the canonical Red Team family. The baseline-canon archive carries the schema and replayable canon; the implementation packet carries the patch-and-validation record; the Prism Observatory carries the doctrinal core; the consolidated pack handles fresh continuations; the bootstrap wrapper handles cold-start re-entry. The public page collapses the five roles into one consolidated page with an explicit lineage appendix so an outside reader can trace the differentiated authorities behind it. Open work: the public-safe export discipline — the rules for what crosses from the internal lineage into the public consolidation — is the next phase. The internal authority structure stays internal; the page above the line stays consolidated.
fields.source_utilization: Source use is organized by role: method authorities, domain evidence, technical background, implementation evidence, and claim limits. The page should show why each source matters; direct quotations and stronger factual claims still require exact passage anchors before publication.
fields.plan: Publish the consolidated public Red Team / Counter-Deception Lab with the lineage appendix that pages the five authority roles to a curious reader without forcing them through forty-five handoff packages. The internal authority structure stays where it belongs. Step 01 Public-safe export discipline Discipline locked for what graduates from the internal authority structure to the public page. Step 02 Lineage appendix Surfaces the five authority roles — Baseline Canon, Implementation, Doctrinal Prism Observatory, One-Upload Continuation, Bootstrap Wrapper — without forcing the reader through forty-five handoff packages. Step 03 Consolidated public page Becomes the consolidated hard-power page the alias-authority map specifies.
fields.status: Status: All five authority roles are present in the canonical Red Team family. The baseline-canon archive carries the schema and replayable canon; the implementation packet carries the patch-and-validation record; the Prism Observatory carries the doctrinal core; the consolidated pack handles fresh continuations; the bootstrap wrapper handles cold-start re-entry. The public page collapses the five roles into one consolidated page with an explicit lineage appendix so an outside reader can trace the differentiated.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: publish the consolidated public Red Team / Counter-Deception Lab with the lineage appendix that pages the five authority roles to a curious reader without forcing them through forty-five handoff packages. The internal authority structure stays where it belongs.
fields.detailed_source_vision: An adversarial-review page for analytical work. The page treats red-teaming as a structured discipline rather than a posture — every analytical claim that leaves the lab gets failure-mode injection, counter-doctrine review, and attack-surface mapping before it goes out. The page exists as the construction counterpart to the analytical pages above: where they produce claims, this page is where those claims are adversarially audited.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: The Red Team family is not one page nor forty unrelated pages — it is a single canonical lab with five differentiated authority roles. Each role answers a specific kind of question; collapsing them into one role or treating them as iterative variants is exactly the failure mode the alias-authority map was written to prevent.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: A consolidated counter-deception lab for adversarial review of analytical work.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It treats red-teaming as a structured discipline with five authority roles: baseline canon, implementation packet, Prism Observatory, continuation pack, and bootstrap wrapper. The lab preserves six-table SQLite schema logic, canonical JSON project schema, replayable canon, patch-and-validation records, doctrinal core, command-registry deltas, cold-start re-entry, and a lineage appendix so a reader can see which authority supports which claim.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It matters because adversarial review is not a posture; it is a system for preserving assumptions, sources, scope, contradictions, patches, doctrine, replay, and re-entry without forcing the public reader through dozens of internal handoff packages.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

researcher_process_map.final_use_case: It matters because adversarial review is not a posture; it is a system for preserving assumptions, sources, scope, contradictions, patches, doctrine, replay, and re-entry without forcing the public reader through dozens of internal handoff packages.
